checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=8339733636.92 (2013-05-26) roughly flat to Goodwill=8460638592.11 (2017-05-28) while revenue fluctuated between ~15.1B and 17.3B in the same window
evidence.location: Goodwill instant series vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet annual series

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=4550765789.28 (2012-05-27) vs IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=4381982471.84 (2017-05-28), a modest decline over 5 years

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=1490419563.58 (2015-05-31) declining to InventoryNet=1367386681.19 (2016-05-29) then rising to InventoryNet=1434996732.13 (2017-05-28), broadly tracking revenue changes

Gross margin computed from CostOfGoodsSold=8545949128.49 and SalesRevenueGoodsNet=14156132497.10 (FY2010, ~39.6%) declining to CostOfGoodsSold=11298422976.30 and SalesRevenueGoodsNet=17052725051.49 (FY2015, ~33.7%), consistent with a large FY2012 acquisition (Goodwill jump from 6529641371.82 (2011-05-29) to 8029443159.93 (2011-08-28)) that changed business mix
evidence.location: CostOfGoodsSold, SalesRevenueGoodsNet annual series; Goodwill instant series

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=346078343.73 (quarter 2016-11-28 to 2017-02-26) jumps to NetIncomeLoss=910559398.51 (quarter 2017-11-27 to 2018-02-25), far exceeding the corresponding OperatingIncomeLoss=573282935.52 for that quarter

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=1316219704.15 (2016-05-29) increasing to ReceivablesNetCurrent=1383249411.31 (2017-05-28), so allowance coverage ratio fell from ~2.18% to ~1.70% of receivables

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Filing chronology shows regular quarterly 10-Q and annual 10-K cadence throughout 2009-2018 with no NT 10-Q/NT 10-K filings in the recent period; only an old NT 11-K filed 2003-11-26 for a benefit plan, unrelated to core financials

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual XBRL data points span from fiscal 2007/2008 through fiscal Q3 2018 (period ending 2018-02-25, filed 2018-03-21) across Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, Receivables, Inventory, Allowance, Cash Flow, and Equity concepts

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction of the bad-debt/doubtful-accounts allowance (a contra-asset reserve) not matched by a proportional decline in gross receivables, which would understate bad debt expense (typically recorded within SG&A) and overstate net receivables and net income for FY2017.

Possible aggressive revenue recognition or extended customer credit terms (channel stuffing) causing receivables to grow even as top-line sales declined, which could indicate revenue pulled forward or recognized ahead of collectability, inflating both revenue and receivables in the period.

rationale_evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet=16020486906.09 (FY2016) declining to SalesRevenueGoodsNet=15108089752.26 (FY2017), a -5.7% decline
ReceivablesNetCurrent grew +5.1% over the same FY2016->FY2017 window despite the revenue decline

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A large non-operating item (likely a one-time deferred tax remeasurement benefit around the 2017 U.S. tax reform, or another below-the-line gain) caused a quarter-over-quarter net income spike disproportionate to operating income and revenue growth, which could mask underlying operating performance trends if not clearly disclosed and isolated.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=910559398.51 (quarter 2017-11-27 to 2018-02-25) vs OperatingIncomeLoss=573282935.52 for the same quarter, a gap far larger than in prior quarters (e.g., NetIncomeLoss=346078343.73 vs OperatingIncomeLoss=524727505.51 in the prior quarter)

top_signals: Receivables grew (+5.1%) while revenue declined (-5.7%) in FY2017, a divergence not seen consistently in earlier years
Allowance for doubtful accounts fell 17.9% (FY2016 to FY2017) even as gross receivables increased, reducing reserve coverage from ~2.18% to ~1.70% of receivables
Unusual net income spike in the quarter ended 2018-02-25 (NetIncomeLoss=910.6M vs OperatingIncomeLoss=573.3M), likely tax-related but warranting disclosure verification

Gross margin compressed from ~40% (FY2010-11) to ~33.7% (FY2015) coincident with a large FY2012 acquisition (Goodwill jump from ~6.5B to ~8.0B), plausibly explaining margin trend but also introducing integration/reserve complexity
Rising leverage: StockholdersEquity fell from ~6.4B (2011) to ~4.2B (2017) while Liabilities grew from ~11.7B to ~15.7B over the same period
